skill.name: product-launch-video
skill.description: Creates product launch and promo videos from a product URL, pasted script, or text brief using the HyperFrames HTML video workflow.
skill.summary: Build product launch videos with captured assets, storyboard planning, motion direction, audio, captions, and MP4 rendering.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: The static findings are partly true positives and partly noisy matches from documentation. I found no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill executes local scripts, invokes external commands, reads provider credentials, captures URLs, and generates HTML that can execute remote or injected script. Publish should wait for remediation of the generated HTML injection and CDN execution risks.

high_findings.title: Generated Caption HTML Allows Script Breakout
high_findings.description: Caption text from audio metadata is inserted into an inline script with JSON.stringify. If untrusted product copy or script text contains a closing script tag sequence, preview or render HTML could execute injected JavaScript.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from caption words into an inline script is clear. I did not find escaping for HTML script termination sequences such as a closing script tag.

medium_findings.title: Remote JavaScript Loaded in Generated Compositions
medium_findings.description: The generated main and caption compositions load GSAP from jsdelivr at render or preview time. This creates a supply chain and deterministic rendering risk, even though the URL targets a known library version.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both generated HTML templates include a hardcoded CDN script tag. The use is functional, but it executes third-party code during the video workflow.

medium_findings.title: Expected External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow intentionally runs npx, node scripts, ffprobe, ffmpeg, and a media engine selected by HF_MEDIA_ENGINE. This is normal for HyperFrames video production, but it increases local execution risk for a community skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and mostly use fixed executables with argument arrays. Risk remains because the workflow runs many local tools and one engine path can be environment selected.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Provider Environment Access
medium_findings.description: The skill uses provider keys for capture and the HeyGen credential for audio services. This appears legitimate, but users should know secrets may be read by the broader HyperFrames media pipeline.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documentation explicitly references API keys and the HeyGen credential. I did not find evidence that this skill exfiltrates secrets itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation Tokens Triggered Many Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Most weak crypto, path traversal, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick alerts in reference markdown are motion-design vocabulary, relative documentation links, CSS values, or prose examples. These are not executable security behavior by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed these files are design guidance and script patterns. The flagged strings do not form shell execution, credential theft, or cryptographic code in these references.

low_findings.title: Project File Writes Are Broad but Expected
low_findings.description: The scripts write frame.md, captions, assets, and index.html under the selected HyperFrames project. This is required for the workflow, but the user should run it only in an intended project directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and generally scoped through the hyperframes directory. The behavior is still important to document because it modifies project files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline Script Data Injection
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated captions place serialized caption groups inside a script tag without an HTML script-safe serializer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The serializer is JSON.stringify inside inline JavaScript. That protects JavaScript syntax but not HTML script tag termination.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Selected Engine Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The audio adapter reads HF_MEDIA_ENGINE and executes the selected path with node.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This can be legitimate for local overrides. It becomes dangerous if an attacker controls the environment or convinces a user to set the variable.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote CDN Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated compositions load a third-party script from jsdelivr during preview or render.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CDN use is direct and confirmed. The URL is a known library location, so the concern is supply chain and reproducibility rather than proven malicious code.

content.user_title: Create Product Launch Videos from Product Pages
content.value_statement: Product teams need launch videos that match the product story, brand, and assets. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through capture, storyboard, motion design, audio, captions, and render steps.
actual_capabilities: Captures a product URL and extracts brand text, assets, colors, and fonts for a launch video.
Creates a frame-by-frame storyboard and narration script for product marketing use cases.
Adopts a HyperFrames design preset and remixes it with captured brand tokens.
Directs each visual frame with time-coded shot sequences and motion vocabulary.
Generates audio metadata, captions, transitions, and an assembled HyperFrames composition.
Runs validation and render commands to produce a final MP4 after user approval.
limitations: It requires the HyperFrames CLI, Node, and the project scripts to run locally.
Best results need a real product URL, clear brief, or approved script.
Audio and music quality depend on available provider credentials or local fallback engines.
It is not intended for PR explainers, generic website tours, or captioning existing footage.

use_cases.title: Launch a SaaS Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a product page and release brief into a short promo with UI moments, proof points, narration, and captions.

use_cases.title: Create a Founder Demo Promo
use_cases.description: Use a concise text brief to produce a polished product story when a full website is not ready yet.

use_cases.title: Package an App Store Reveal
use_cases.description: Convert app copy, screenshots, and brand direction into a launch video with motion-led feature beats.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Promo
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt with a product URL and simple direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 45 second product launch video for this product URL: [URL]. Use a clear value proposition, captions, and a direct call to action.

prompt_templates.title: Script First Launch Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a prepared script while still using the site for brand assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product launch video from this script and product URL. Keep the core wording, but improve pacing for a 60 second narrated promo: [SCRIPT] [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Audience Specific Feature Reveal
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus the story on one user group and a feature outcome.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 75 second feature reveal for [PRODUCT]. Target [AUDIENCE]. Emphasize [FEATURE], show the workflow, and end with [CTA].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brand Directed Launch
prompt_templates.scenario: Provide constraints for structure, format, voice, and visual style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product launch video for [URL] in [FORMAT]. Use a restrained B2B tone, a problem to proof to CTA arc, captured assets only, and a cinematic motion system.

output_examples.input: A SaaS homepage URL with a request for a 60 second launch promo.
output_examples.output: A HyperFrames project with captured brand assets, an approved storyboard, narration, captions, transitions, validation results, and a rendered MP4.

output_examples.input: A pasted launch script and a request to keep the wording mostly intact.
output_examples.output: A storyboard that preserves the message, splits the script into paced frames, adds visual direction, and prepares the video for rendering.

output_examples.input: A product brief with no website capture target.
output_examples.output: A no-capture project using the brief as source material, with generated planning files and a complete visual production workflow.

best_practices: Provide a product URL, target audience, desired length, aspect ratio, and primary call to action before starting.
Approve the storyboard before frame building, because later steps depend on the locked story and asset choices.
Run HyperFrames lint, validate, inspect, and snapshot before rendering the final MP4.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for generic website tours when the goal is not a launch or promo video.
Do not ask it to caption or edit existing footage. Use the dedicated captioning or recut workflows instead.
Do not run it in a sensitive directory because it creates and modifies project files.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: A product URL plus a short brief works best. A finished script can also be used when wording must stay close.

faq.question: Can it work without a website?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use a no-capture path with a pasted brief or script, but visual assets must be described or supplied.

faq.question: Does it generate the final video file?
faq.answer: Yes, after validation and user approval. The workflow renders an MP4 through HyperFrames.

faq.question: Does it need API keys?
faq.answer: Some capture, narration, music, and sound effects paths use provider credentials. It can continue offline with reduced media features.

faq.question: Is this a general website video skill?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on product launch and promotional videos. General website tours should use a website video workflow.

faq.question: What security review result applies?
faq.answer: The audit found high risk from generated HTML script injection and remote script loading. It should be remediated before marketplace publishing.
